methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.0B last post money.

data.narrative:
ICON, a Series C-stage housing and construction company founded by Kennan Frost, carries a current post-money valuation of $2.0B following $450M+ in total capital raised. The absence of directly comparable public or private peers in the provided dataset limits traditional multiples-based triangulation for this sector.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology was applied to establish a forward-looking valuation range. The Series C post-money of $2.0B, when adjusted by a typical 1.7x multiplier reflective of Series C to later-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $3.4B. This single applicable method generates a triangulated range of $2.9B (low) to $3.9B (high), with a median point estimate of $3.4B.

The range reflects reasonable spacing around the stage-progression output but is anchored to a limited methodological foundation. The absence of comparable company data—neither public sector benchmarks nor private peer sets—represents a material constraint on validation. Any refinement of this valuation would require additional comp universe data, granular unit economics, or forward revenue/margin projections specific to ICON's operating profile. The $2.9B to $3.9B range is defensible given current inputs but carries elevated uncertainty pending disclosure of peer valuations or operational metrics.
